all_sql_queries.sql: SELECT d.DESCRIPTION AS DEVICE_DESCRIPTION, COUNT(*) AS IMPLANT_COUNT, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT c.DESCRIPTION, ';
SELECT d.DESCRIPTION AS DEVICE_DESCRIPTION, COUNT(*) AS IMPLANT_COUNT, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT c.DESCRIPTION, ';
SELECT ID, FIRST FROM PATIENTS (not select id, first from patients)
SELECT * FROM PATIENTS WHERE ID = 'patient_id';
SELECT * FROM CONDITIONS WHERE PATIENT = 'patient_id';
SELECT * FROM CONDITIONS WHERE DESCRIPTION ILIKE '%diabetes%';
SELECT P.FIRST, C.DESCRIPTION FROM PATIENTS P JOIN CONDITIONS C ON P.ID = C.PATIENT;

SELECT statements • Use ILIKE for case-insensitive searches • Column names are case-sensitive - use UPPERCASE • Proper JOIN syntax: ON table1.column = table2.column === RELEVANT TABLE INFO 2 === TABLE: PROVIDERS ================================================== Table: Providers This table holds provider details including name, specialty, and location. Columns: – ID (unique provider identifier) – ORGANIZATION (FK → organizations.ID) – NAME (provider name) – GENDER (provider gender) – SPECIALITY (clinical specialty) – ADDRESS (street address) – CITY (city) – STATE (state) – ZIP (postal code) – LAT (latitude) – LON (longitude) Identification fields: ID Relation fields: providers.ID ↔ encounters.PROVIDER organizations.ID ↔ providers.ORGANIZATION 🔥 CRITICAL COLUMN NAME RULES: - ALL COLUMN NAMES ARE UPPERCASE (ID, FIRST, LAST, etc.) - NEVER use lowercase column names (id, first, last) - ALWAYS refer to columns exactly as shown above 💡 POSTGRESQL SYNTAX REQUIREMENTS: - Use uppercase column names as shown in schema - Use proper JOIN syntax with ON conditions - Always include LIMIT for SELECT statements - Use ILIKE for case-insensitive text searches 📝 CORRECT SAMPLE QUERIES: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM PROVIDERS;
SELECT * FROM PROVIDERS LIMIT 10;
SELECT P.FIRST, C.DESCRIPTION FROM PATIENTS P JOIN CONDITIONS C ON P.ID = C.PATIENT;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM PROVIDERS;
SELECT * FROM PROVIDERS LIMIT 10;
SELECT D.DESCRIPTION AS DEVICE_DESCRIPTION, COUNT(*) AS IMPLANT_COUNT, STRING_AGG(DISTINCT C.DESCRIPTION, ';
